ddls:
CREATE TABLE "markets" (
  "market_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each market */,
  "market_name" TEXT /* Name of the market (e.g., DJIA, Nasdaq, S&P 500) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the market */,
  "market_type" TEXT /* Type of market (e.g., stock, bond, commodity) */,
  "sector" TEXT /* Sector of the market (e.g., technology, finance, healthcare) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("market_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "market_id" INTEGER /* ID of the market the data belongs to */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the market data */,
  "open_price" REAL /* Opening price of the market on the given date */,
  "high_price" REAL /* High price of the market on the given date */,
  "low_price" REAL /* Low price of the market on the given date */,
  "close_price" REAL /* Closing price of the market on the given date */,
  "trading_volume" INTEGER /* Trading volume of the market on the given date */,
  "price_change" REAL /* Price change of the market on the given date */,
  "monthly_change" REAL /* Monthly change in the market price */,
  "quarterly_change" REAL /* Quarterly change in the market price */,
  "ytd_change" REAL /* Year-to-date change in the market price */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_data_market_id FOREIGN KEY ("market_id") REFERENCES markets ("market_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_data" (
  "historical_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical data point */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the historical market data */,
  "open_price" REAL /* Opening price of the market on the given date */,
  "high_price" REAL /* High price of the market on the given date */,
  "low_price" REAL /* Low price of the market on the given date */,
  "close_price" REAL /* Closing price of the market on the given date */,
  "trading_volume" INTEGER /* Trading volume of the market on the given date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("historical_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend */,
  "market_id" INTEGER /* ID of the market the trend belongs to */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the trend */,
  "trend_type" TEXT /* Type of trend (e.g., upward, downward, stable) */,
  "trend_value" REAL /* Value of the trend */,
  "trend_direction" TEXT /* Direction of the trend (e.g., upward, downward) */,
  "trend_strength" TEXT /* Strength of the trend (e.g., strong, weak) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_trends_market_id FOREIGN KEY ("market_id") REFERENCES markets ("market_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trend_predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* ID of the trend the prediction belongs to */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the prediction */,
  "predicted_trend" TEXT /* Predicted trend */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "market_id" INTEGER /* ID of the market the alert belongs to */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., price movement, trend change) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Level of the alert (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "message_type" TEXT /* Type of alert message (e.g., price movement, trend change) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_alerts_market_id FOREIGN KEY ("market_id") REFERENCES markets ("market_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert the notification belongs to */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the notification */,
  "notification_message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, investor, admin) */,
  "company" TEXT /* Company of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each preference */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the preference belongs to */,
  "preference_name" TEXT /* Name of the preference */,
  "preference_value" TEXT /* Value of the preference */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the market data */,
  "market_id" INTEGER /* ID of the market being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the market data was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the market data was accessed */,
  "access_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_market_id FOREIGN KEY ("market_id") REFERENCES markets ("market_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_activity_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log */,
  "access_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access event */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date of the log */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message of the log */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_hierarchy" (
  "hierarchy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hierarchy */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "parent_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent role */,
  "hierarchy_level" INTEGER /* Level of the hierarchy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hierarchy_id")
)
